subject: Feature upgrade for Sodium

Feature upgrade for Sodium

Add Sodium SR3 compatibility to SDN-R features

entries.message:
Patch Set 1: Verified+1

Build Successful 

https://jenkins.onap.org/job/ccsdk-features-maven-verify-master-mvn35-openjdk8/202/ : SUCCESS

Logs: https://logs.onap.org/production/vex-yul-ecomp-jenkins-1/ccsdk-features-maven-verify-master-mvn35-openjdk8/202

Patch Set 1:

Neon and Sodium compatibility of SDN-R apps.
Please merge.

Patch Set 1:

Its a big change, can it be done in multiple chunks.

Patch Set 1: Code-Review-1

(2 comments)

Reviews should generally be less than 2K lines of code.  This is over 28K and over 500 files changed.
I'll do my best to review, but it is going to take weeks.

Patch Set 1:

This check-in is intended to provide Sodium SR3 compatibility of the SDN-R applications. You requested this indirectly via comment to https://gerrit.onap.org/r/c/ccsdk/parent/+/109831.
So the code is mainly adjusting to Sodium and tested with the parent provided by Micha. 
Additionally there was also the action to fix the "check-style" problem that we had. Unfortunately there are a lot of changes because "tabs" are replaced by "spaces". We like to improve here to "not" getting such feedback that to many has changed.

To avoid incompatibility I had to remove to bundles. This bundles are containing yang-tools generated classes. This classes are not compatible between Neon and Sodium. For this reason I had removed the related bundle with all the generated classes. Looks as a big change. But was necessary to provide the requested Neon/Sodium compatibility of the code. This were about 150 deletions. 

This code is tested now with local Sodium builds and the "expected" parents that are provided via CCSDK-2518 and Neon builds. 
The Easticsearch version that you commented is used for a plugin that is doing JUnit tests and is not used in the running Environment. Proposal is to change this version till M1 to avoid hickups.

Patch Set 1:

> Patch Set 1:
> 
> Its a big change, can it be done in multiple chunks.

Yes, I understand you concern. But in the sum of changes would not be smaller at the end. I hope to find a way of listing more of the background:
- There are 150 deletions of devicemanager-gran with checked-in yang-tools generated classes .. Sodium creates them different than Neon. I needed to remove this part during migration in ONAP.
- Most (95%) changes are adjustments of wrong Tabs to spaces to be in line with ONAP Code style rules.
- Further (5%) adjustments are for Junit test, using ODL yang-tools generated classes, needing update to use mockit .. because Sodium yang-tools are creating classes differently.
- My Collegue Micha was sending the "parent" to Sodium change on it's way .. CCSDK-2518 / https://gerrit.onap.org/r/c/ccsdk/parent/+/109831 .. Dan requested to provide "Sodium/Neon" compliant code. So we provided this code .. and Micha was verifying the status of CCSDK/Repos. There is a lot of migration work to do. A reasonable part of the code is not "compatible" with Sodium and needs changes. We are concentrating on this part and are hoping to get this first piece of Sodium code into gerrit.

Patch Set 1:

I will now split "Tab/Space" task to "ONAP Code style rules" to a different jira.
And restart Sodium changes later with smaller pieces again .. it will need some time.
